memories: User has a severe allergy to peanuts.
User's home office doubles as a music room, where the ukulele shares wall space with three guitars and a vintage record player.
User despises raw carrots but will eat them roasted with honey.
User's two mothers call every Tuesday evening, often asking about Leo but never pressing for details.
User has a tradition of making homemade pizza every Friday night, carefully checking all toppings for peanut oil cross-contamination.
User is learning to play the ukulele.
User is still deeply hurt after their childhood best friend, Mark, betrayed their trust by revealing a deeply personal secret to a group of mutual friends, leading to the end of their friendship.
User recently joined a recreational soccer league to combat low energy and has found that the post-game pub gatherings are surprisingly enjoyable.
User's son, Leo, is starting kindergarten and is very shy.
query: My son is starting kindergarten soon and he's very shy. I want to help him learn how to make friends and navigate social situations. What are some key lessons I should teach him?
